manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: engine, ambulance, rome, figure, iron, cook, pole, crash, draft.
Your opponent's team words are: spine, green, novel, chocolate, hole, boom, mexico, disease.
Distractor words are: england, poison, cell, embassy, track, revolution, plate, bugle.
The kill words are: .

message.reasoning:
We need to produce a clue (single word) and a list of target words that are our team's words from the given list: engine, ambulance, rome, figure, iron, cook, pole, crash, draft. That's nine words. We can choose any subset; presumably we want to maximize coverage while still being plausible for partner to guess. The clue must be semantically related to those target words. Must not be any of the words in any list, and cannot contain parts of them. Also cannot be a kill word (none given). We need to produce: "CLUE: <WORD>" and "TARGETS: <list>" with comma-separated list.

We need to consider which clue could relate to many of our team words. Our team words: engine, ambulance, rome, figure, iron, cook, pole, crash, draft. The opponent words: spine, green, novel, chocolate, hole, boom, mexico, disease. Distractor: england, poison, cell, embassy, track, revolution, plate, bugle. We need a clue that is not any of those words or contain parts (like "eng" appears in engine; we cannot use "engine"? Not allowed to be exactly the same, but "engine" is a team word, not allowed. Also can't contain parts of them? The rule: "it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them." So the clue cannot be "engine" (that's a team word). Also cannot contain parts of them, like "eng" is part of "engine", "ambul" part of "ambulance". Also "rome" part of "room"? But "room" is not in lists. However it's safer not to use any substrings. Also cannot be "iron" (team). So we need something like "metal"? That's related to "iron" and maybe "engine" (contains metal), "pole"? Not exactly. Could use "vehicle"? But that's not directly a part. Actually "vehicle" relates to "engine", "ambulance". Also "crash" maybe. Could use "road"? "Road" is related to "ambulance", "engine", "crash", "pole"? Not so much. "Transport"? That could include "engine", "ambulance". Also "draft" could be "air". "Figure" could be "shape". "Cook" could be "food". "Pole" could be "north". "Rome" is a city. "Iron" is a metal.

We can think of grouping by categories: The words can be grouped by categories: "vehicle" (engine, ambulance, crash). "city" (Rome). "metal" (iron). "food" (cook). "shape" (figure). "pole" (north?). "draft" maybe "air" or "wind". Some of these are not obviously connected. However a clue like "metal" could target "iron" and maybe "engine" (makes engine from metal). Not sure.

We need a single word clue that relates to multiple of our team words. The clue must be semantically related; not too obscure. So we could pick something like "vehicle" to cover engine, ambulance, maybe crash (as accidents). "Vehicle" doesn't include any of the words in the list; it's not a part of any? "Vehicle" does not contain any of the words (engine, ambulance, etc.) Let's check: "engine" contains "eng", "ambulance" contains "amb". "vehicle" is distinct. So it's allowed. "Vehicle" would be a good clue. What words would we list as targets? Could be engine, ambulance, crash (maybe). Also "draft"? Not really. "Pole"? Not related. "Figure"? Not. "Cook"? No. "Rome"? No. "Iron"? Not exactly. But perhaps "metal" could be more broad: iron is metal, engine is metal, ambulance includes metal, crash involves metal. Also "pole" could be metal? Not really. "Figure" maybe shape of metal? Not. "Cook"? Not. "Draft"? Not. "Rome"? Not. So "metal" would target iron, engine, ambulance, crash maybe, maybe pole (metal pole). That could be a decent grouping: iron, engine, ambulance, crash, pole. That's 5 words. Could also include "figure" (like figure of metal?). But maybe not.

What about "shape"? Could target figure (shape), pole (shape), maybe "draft"? Not sure.

Maybe "speed"? Could target engine, ambulance, crash, draft (draft as speed?), but not.

We could use "transport". That's similar to vehicle. That would target engine, ambulance, maybe crash (as accident). Could also target "draft"? No. "Pole"? No. "Figure"? No.

Maybe use "accident"? That would target crash, ambulance maybe, but also could target engine? Not really. Not great.

Perhaps grouping by first letters: The words share some letters?
